title: Chenopodium overi
intro:
There is no information on Chenopodium overi. However, I can provide information on Chenopodium ambrosioides, also known as Mexican tea or epazote, which belongs to the same genus. 

Chenopodium ambrosioides is an annual herbaceous plant native to Mexico and Central America. 

Its health benefits include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, which can aid in the treatment of digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin conditions. 

Bioactive constituents of Chenopodium ambrosioides include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and essential oils, such as limonene and beta-pinene, which contribute to its medicinal properties.

The most common herbal preparations made with Chenopodium ambrosioides include teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to alleviate symptoms of indigestion, nausea, and menstrual cramps.
